steps.title: Identify the passage's two-part structure
steps.content: The passage describes a map collection and then explains how it was made—consulting existing maps and interviewing travelers.

steps.title: Match the purpose and eliminate the distractors
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To discuss the benefits of studying mapmaking’: the passage never discusses benefits of studying mapmaking. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To explain how travelers created maps’: al-Idrisi created the maps; travelers only supplied stories he consulted. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To describe a collection of medieval maps and how it was created’: captures both the description of The Book of Roger and the account of al-Idrisi's process. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘To compare medieval Arabic and Greek mapmaking techniques’: the passage mentions Arabic and Greek maps as sources but never compares techniques. ✗
